meta.title: GPS As An Attack Vector
meta.description:
Almost all ICS rely on a GPS clock to provide good time. Mike Toecker of Context Industrial Security looks at if this ubiquitous component could be used as an attack path to the ICS. This is not trying to spoof a GPS clock, althoughh it does leverage the fact that the GPS signal is not an authenticated signal. 

Mike is suggesting it could be possible to create and use a command and control channel through a modified GPS clock. A way of getting into not normally accessible ICS. Proof of concept is not there yet, and Mike goes into the proposed architecture, the development path, and some of the challenges to do this. He is transparent on what is done and what remains to be done. Lots of technical detail in this session. Who is going to help make this happen?
